summary: wrapWithProvenance must never stringify a non-JSON body into results; surface an auth error for an HTML login page and an API error otherwise.
reason: The provenance envelope's json.Valid==false branch silently coerced an invalid body to a string and shipped it as results with a success exit — the mechanism that let an HTTP-200 HTML login page reach the user as data. Backstop the generic read-path guard so no read command can emit non-JSON as results with exit 0, even if a future path misses the upstream guard.
validated_outcome: wrapWithProvenance(HTML) returns a code-4 auth error and nil envelope; non-JSON garbage returns a code-5 API error; valid JSON still wraps into {meta,results}. Unit tests in auth_html_guard_test.go cover all three. go build/vet/test pass.
